title: Raid at Bakura
content:

|next=[[Battle of Geonosis (Galactic Civil War)|Third Battle Of Geonosis]] |image=[[File:RaidOnBakura-RSIII.jpg]] |name=Raid at Bakura |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] |date=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]The Raid at Bakura follows the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. |place=[[Bakura Imperial Prison]], [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] |result=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] victory **[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] forces recover [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] **Imperials capture the passengers on a transport |side1=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side2=Alliance to Restore the Republic |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sarkli]] |commanders2=*[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] *[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*85 [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] *Imperial prison defensive [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] *3 [[Imperial Action transport|Imperial Action transports]] *[[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos]] *1 [[Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)|Imperial escort carrier]] {{C|late battle}} |forces2=*1 [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] *[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] *3 [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]] *1 stolen [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Heavy loss of TIE fighters *Loss of turbolaser defenses *4 Imperial Action transports disabled |casual2=*1 medium transport *At least 4 starfighters **Hobbie's X-wing |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|This isn't good.|Wedge Antilles spots the aftermath of the orbital battle|}} The '''Raid at Bakura''', also known as the '''First Battle of Bakura''', occurred in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] when the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] discovered that a number of [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] captured from [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] were being held aboard an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bakura Imperial Prison|detention center]] orbiting [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. ==Prelude== Having weathered the loss of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] set about rebuilding their infrastructure. The [[Ace/Legends|ace]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] were sent to [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] to rescue captured [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]]. ==The battle== When the squadron arrived, three [[Imperial Action transport|Imperial Dropship Transports]] were already exiting the prison facility with the scientists. [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], in his [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]], disabled a fleeing Imperial transport carrying a batch of scientists with his [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]]. A [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] docked with the Imperial ship and evacuated the prisoners. A number of TIE fighters were patrolling the area, and upon receiving a battle alert rushed towards the battle scene. After completing its docking, the first Rebel transport moved at top speed away from the battle to escape safely into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Several TIEs gave chase, but Antilles drove them off. As that happened, a second rescue transport was inbound. Antilles then disabled another dropship transport to recover the prisoners. After the third dropship transport was disabled, a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] disabled [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian's]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], sending him crashing onto the surface of the planet. Antilles docked with the prison to steal a [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bomber]] to pick him up, as the rescue craft required an accommodation for two. Antilles had to dodge the hundreds of Bakura surface heavy missile batteries and turrets littering the surface. Using his TIE bomber's [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], he managed to break through the defenses and pickup Hobbie. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike}} Just before picking up Hobbie, Wedge picked up some [[advanced proton bomb]]s from one of the domed towers that he destroyed. {{Endgame}} Upon arrival back in orbit, Antilles noticed that debris littered the area. [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] informed him that an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)|escort carrier]] made off with a batch of scientists and destroyed one of the transports and several Rebel starfighters, and it seemed it was headed toward [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] due to its communication log. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|The situation is well in hand. The Rebels still do not grasp the scope of our plans.
Yes, my master, he is resourceful.|Palpatine and Vader discuss Sarkli's successful retrieval of the scientists at Bakura|}} Determined to rescue the scientists, Rogue Squadron would lead an assault team to Geonosis. Likewise, Emperor Palpatine explains to Vader that the Rebels were still unaware of the scope of their plans, implying that the one responsible was [[Sarkli|one of their own]]. The Rebels eventually tracked the abductor to Geonosis via a transmission, and immediately departed to rescue the scientists. Unfortunately, the lead turned out to be a [[Battle of Geonosis (Galactic Civil War)|trap]], forcing the other Rebel transports to evacuate without rescuing the scientists. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Attack the Imperial holding facility orbiting Bakura. Disable the Imperial transports, and give the Rebel evacuation teams time to offload the prisoners from Hoth.|Mission briefing for "Raid at Bakura"|}} The Raid at Bakura level in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' was created largely to allow the player to make their own decisions, a method that likewise required script modulators to lessen the difficulty. The space station was based on a rough concept based on some sci-fi movies they saw, and was rendered to be more in-line with the ''Star Wars'' universe. Flying the TIE bomber in the mission is a mini-game unlocked upon reaching silver medal status. Destroying the missile launchers within a certain time limit is necessary to unlock the TIE Bomber as a flyable craft. Alternatively, if the player receives bronze level status, an FMV will play showing arrival of the Imperial Escort carrier and the re-capture of the scientists and the destruction of the Rebel Transport. It was largely created as a reward for the player for putting up with the difficulties of choosing their own path in the mission. This article assumes the full mission was achieved. Procuring the upgrade will fulfill the bonus objective for the mission. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW 3 ABY 2}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Bakura (3 NSY) |es=Incursión en Bakura }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Bakura, Raid at}} [[Category:Battles of Bakura]] [[Category:Battles of the Galactic Civil War]] [[Category:Rescue missions]]
